channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: PRRI Public Fellows Program 2026–2027: Receive Up to $25,000 in Funding, National Recognition, and Research Support for Scholars Studying Religion, Politics, and Culture
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
The Public Religion Research Institute (PRRI) has officially opened applications for its highly competitive 2026–2027 Public Fellows Program, an exceptional opportunity designed for mid-career scholars seeking to amplify the public impact of their research. Researchers working in the fields of religion, politics, culture, democracy, immigration, gender, and LGBTQ issues are invited to apply for this […]

The post PRRI Public Fellows Program 2026–2027: Receive Up to $25,000 in Funding, National Recognition, and Research Support for Scholars Studying Religion, Politics, and Culture appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Public Religion Research Institute (PRRI) has officially opened applications for its highly competitive 2026–2027 Public Fellows Program, an exceptional opportunity designed for mid-career scholars seeking to amplify the public impact of their research.

Researchers working in the fields of religion, politics, culture, democracy, immigration, gender, and LGBTQ issues are invited to apply for this prestigious fellowship, which provides financial support, collaborative research opportunities, professional development, and direct engagement with leading scholars and public intellectuals.

Applications are now being accepted for the next cohort of PRRI Public Fellows, with submissions due by Friday, June 19, 2026.

About the PRRI Public Fellows Program

Established in 2017, the PRRI Public Fellows Program was created to bridge the gap between academic research and public discourse.

While scholars often produce groundbreaking research, much of this work remains confined to academic journals, conferences, and specialized audiences. PRRI seeks to change that reality by helping researchers effectively communicate their findings to policymakers, journalists, civic leaders, community stakeholders, and the broader public.

The fellowship is an 11-month, non-residential program that equips scholars with the skills, mentorship, resources, and networks necessary to transform rigorous academic research into accessible public scholarship.

Participants learn how to shape their expertise into impactful commentary, public analysis, media engagement, and thought leadership that can influence important conversations taking place across society.

Why the PRRI Public Fellows Program Matters

Today’s social and political landscape is increasingly shaped by complex issues involving religion, democracy, immigration, gender, identity, and cultural change.

Researchers possess valuable evidence and insights that can help inform public understanding and policy discussions. However, effectively communicating these findings beyond academic audiences requires specialized skills and support.

The PRRI Public Fellows Program provides scholars with an opportunity to:

  • Expand the reach of their research.
  • Influence public conversations.
  • Engage policymakers and journalists.
  • Develop public-facing communication skills.
  • Build interdisciplinary collaborations.
  • Strengthen their professional networks.
  • Produce research with measurable public impact.

The program recognizes that evidence-based scholarship can play a critical role in fostering informed democratic dialogue and addressing some of society’s most pressing challenges.

Research Areas for the 2026–2027 Cohort

PRRI will select a diverse cohort of nine scholars whose work aligns with one or more of the institute’s major research priorities.

1. Pluralism and Democracy

This research area explores questions related to:

  • Democratic participation
  • Civic engagement
  • Political institutions
  • Religious diversity
  • Social cohesion
  • Democratic norms and values
  • Public trust and governance

Scholars examining how diverse societies navigate democratic challenges are encouraged to apply.

2. Immigration and American Society

Researchers studying immigration-related topics may focus on:

  • Immigration policy
  • Social integration
  • Identity and belonging
  • Public opinion
  • Community development
  • Migration trends
  • Cultural adaptation
  • Economic and political impacts of immigration

PRRI is particularly interested in research that contributes to a deeper understanding of immigration’s role in contemporary society.

3. LGBTQ Rights and Gender Politics

This area supports scholars investigating:

  • Gender equality
  • LGBTQ rights
  • Gender identity
  • Public attitudes toward LGBTQ communities
  • Policy development
  • Social movements
  • Political representation
  • Religious perspectives on gender and sexuality

Research that advances understanding of evolving social and political debates around gender and LGBTQ issues is strongly encouraged.

Fellowship Benefits and Funding

One of the most attractive aspects of the PRRI Public Fellows Program is the substantial support available to selected fellows.

$5,000 Fellowship Stipend

Every fellow receives a direct stipend of:

$5,000

This funding helps support participation in the fellowship and related scholarly activities.

Up to $10,000 in Course Release Funding

Selected fellows may access:

Up to $10,000 in course release funds

These resources are intended to provide scholars with additional time to focus on research, writing, collaboration, and public engagement activities during the Spring 2027 semester.

Up to $10,000 in Collaborative Research Microgrants

The program also offers:

Up to $10,000 in microgrant funding

These grants support the design and implementation of collaborative research projects among fellows and other partners.

This funding creates opportunities for innovative interdisciplinary scholarship and expanded research impact.

Fully Funded In-Person Convening in Washington, D.C.

Public Fellows participate in an expenses-paid gathering hosted by PRRI in Washington, D.C.

Benefits include:

  • Networking opportunities
  • Professional development
  • Collaboration with fellow scholars
  • Engagement with PRRI leadership
  • Research presentations and discussions
  • Exposure to policy and media communities

The convening serves as a cornerstone of the fellowship experience and helps build lasting professional relationships among participants.

What Fellows Will Learn

Throughout the fellowship, participants receive support to strengthen their ability to communicate research beyond academic audiences.

Key areas of development may include:

Public Scholarship

Learning how to translate complex research findings into accessible content for broader audiences.

Media Engagement

Understanding how to work effectively with journalists and media outlets.

Policy Communication

Developing strategies to share evidence-based findings with policymakers and decision-makers.

Public Writing

Improving the ability to write op-eds, essays, public reports, and commentary pieces.

Thought Leadership

Building a public profile as a trusted expert in a specialized field of research.

Collaborative Research

Working with fellow scholars to create impactful interdisciplinary projects.

Who Should Apply?

The fellowship is ideal for scholars who:

  • Want their research to reach wider audiences.
  • Seek greater public impact from their academic work.
  • Are interested in public scholarship and civic engagement.
  • Conduct research related to religion, politics, culture, democracy, immigration, gender, or LGBTQ issues.
  • Wish to collaborate with scholars from diverse disciplines.
  • Want to strengthen their communication and public engagement skills.

Selection of the 2026–2027 Cohort

PRRI aims to create a diverse cohort of nine fellows representing a range of disciplinary backgrounds, methodological approaches, perspectives, and research interests.

The fellowship values diversity of thought and encourages applications from scholars whose work contributes to meaningful public conversations.

How to Apply

Interested applicants should review the complete application requirements and prepare all required materials before submission.

Applicants are encouraged to carefully review eligibility criteria, fellowship expectations, and application instructions provided by PRRI.

Official Information:
PRRI.org

Application Deadline

Friday, June 19, 2026

Applications submitted after the deadline may not be considered.

Final Thoughts

The PRRI Public Fellows Program represents a unique opportunity for mid-career scholars who want to expand the influence of their research beyond academia.

Through generous funding, professional development opportunities, collaborative research support, and direct engagement with public audiences, the fellowship helps researchers transform scholarly knowledge into meaningful social impact.

For academics studying religion, politics, democracy, immigration, culture, gender, and LGBTQ issues, this program offers an exceptional platform to strengthen public engagement, build influential networks, and contribute to critical national conversations.

Interested scholars are encouraged to apply before the June 19, 2026 deadline and become part of the next generation of PRRI Public Fellows.

 

Apply here 

 

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity, Click here
  • More global Fellowship opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here

The post PRRI Public Fellows Program 2026–2027: Receive Up to $25,000 in Funding, National Recognition, and Research Support for Scholars Studying Religion, Politics, and Culture appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: University of Oxford Opens Applications for Quantitative Research Assistant Position 2026
item.dc:creator: Akua Ntim
item.description:
The University of Oxford has officially opened applications for the Quantitative Research Assistant position under the Oxford Department of International Development (ODID). This opportunity offers aspiring and experienced researchers the chance to work within one of the world’s most respected academic institutions while contributing to impactful international development research. The position is ideal for candidates […]

The post University of Oxford Opens Applications for Quantitative Research Assistant Position 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The University of Oxford has officially opened applications for the Quantitative Research Assistant position under the Oxford Department of International Development (ODID). This opportunity offers aspiring and experienced researchers the chance to work within one of the world’s most respected academic institutions while contributing to impactful international development research.

The position is ideal for candidates interested in research, policy analysis, data interpretation, global development, public policy, economics, health systems, social sciences, and evidence-based decision-making. Successful applicants will become part of a highly collaborative and intellectually stimulating environment known for producing world-class research with global influence.

The University of Oxford continues to maintain a strong reputation for academic excellence, innovation, diversity, and inclusion. Through this role, selected candidates will have the opportunity to contribute to meaningful projects while strengthening their research and professional experience within a globally recognized institution.

About the Quantitative Research Assistant Role

The Quantitative Research Assistant role is designed for individuals with strong analytical and communication skills who are passionate about research and data-driven impact. The position sits within the Oxford Department of International Development, a department recognized internationally for its contribution to development studies and interdisciplinary research.

As a Quantitative Research Assistant, the successful candidate will support research activities involving quantitative methods, data analysis, interpretation of findings, communication of research outcomes, and collaboration with researchers and stakeholders. Candidates are expected to possess excellent interpersonal and verbal communication skills, especially the ability to communicate complex information to both specialist and non-specialist audiences.

This role provides an excellent platform for professionals and graduates seeking to advance their careers in academia, development research, economics, policy evaluation, public health, or international development.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Working at the University of Oxford provides more than just employment. It offers access to a world-leading research ecosystem where innovation, critical thinking, and collaboration are deeply valued.

Research assistants at Oxford gain exposure to:

  • Internationally recognized research projects
  • Global policy discussions and development debates
  • Advanced research methodologies
  • Collaborative academic networks
  • Professional mentorship opportunities
  • Multidisciplinary working environments
  • Career advancement pathways in research and academia

The experience acquired through this position can significantly strengthen future applications for postgraduate studies, fellowships, research grants, policy roles, doctoral programs, and international development careers.

Salary and Employment Benefits

According to the official job listing, the salary range for this position falls within the Research Grade 6 scale, with annual compensation ranging from £35,681 to £41,636.

Beyond salary, the University of Oxford is widely recognized for providing a supportive and inclusive work environment. Employees often benefit from:

  • Professional development opportunities
  • Access to world-class academic resources
  • Inclusive and diverse workplace culture
  • Networking opportunities with global scholars
  • Exposure to impactful international research
  • Long-term career growth potential

Oxford also emphasizes equality, diversity, and inclusion. The department openly encourages applications from all eligible candidates regardless of background, identity, race, or nationality.

Oxford’s Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion

One of the standout aspects of this opportunity is the University’s clear commitment to equality and inclusion. The Oxford Department of International Development highlights that it actively embraces diversity and works to create a welcoming environment for all employees.

The department has received recognition through the Athena Swan Bronze Award for efforts aimed at promoting organizational and cultural change that supports equality and diversity. It is also taking active measures to promote race equality and reduce discrimination and bias within the academic environment.

This commitment makes the role especially attractive for international applicants and underrepresented groups seeking opportunities within globally respected institutions.

Application Requirements

Applicants will be required to submit the following documents:

  • A detailed Curriculum Vitae (CV)
  • A supporting statement

The supporting statement is a critical part of the application process and should clearly demonstrate how the applicant meets the requirements of the role. Candidates are encouraged to highlight relevant academic achievements, research experience, analytical skills, communication abilities, and professional accomplishments.

Applicants should ensure that all documents are properly prepared and submitted before the application deadline.

How to Apply

Applications must be submitted online through the official University of Oxford recruitment portal. Interested candidates should carefully review the job description and prepare all necessary application documents in advance.

Applicants are encouraged to apply early to avoid last-minute technical issues or submission delays.

Important Deadline

The deadline for submitting applications is:

10 June 2026 at 12:00 noon (UK time)

Late applications may not be considered, so applicants are advised to complete and submit their applications well before the closing time.

Final Thoughts

The University of Oxford Quantitative Research Assistant Position 2026 represents an outstanding opportunity for researchers and professionals seeking meaningful international experience within a globally prestigious institution.

Whether you are looking to strengthen your research career, contribute to impactful development work, expand your professional network, or gain experience in one of the world’s top universities, this position provides a strong platform for growth and advancement.

APPLY HERE

For more information on this opportunity, click here.

For more global opportunities, click here.

 

 

The post University of Oxford Opens Applications for Quantitative Research Assistant Position 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: FAO Investment Support Officer Job in Ghana 2026: Fully Detailed Guide for International Applicants
item.dc:creator: Akua Ntim
item.description:
The Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO) has officially opened applications for the position of Investment Support Officer based in Accra, Ghana. This opportunity is ideal for professionals interested in international development, agricultural transformation, rural investment, food systems, and global policy implementation. As one of the leading agencies within the United Nations […]

The post FAO Investment Support Officer Job in Ghana 2026: Fully Detailed Guide for International Applicants appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO) has officially opened applications for the position of Investment Support Officer based in Accra, Ghana. This opportunity is ideal for professionals interested in international development, agricultural transformation, rural investment, food systems, and global policy implementation.

As one of the leading agencies within the United Nations system, FAO plays a critical role in addressing global food insecurity, sustainable agriculture, climate resilience, and rural development. The newly announced Investment Support Officer position offers professionals the chance to contribute directly to impactful agricultural and economic development projects while working within an internationally respected organization.

The position is categorized as a Professional-level staff role under the FAO organizational structure and presents a strong opportunity for individuals seeking long-term international development careers.

About FAO

The Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations is a global institution dedicated to defeating hunger and improving food security across the world. FAO works with governments, development institutions, farmers, researchers, and international stakeholders to build resilient food systems and improve livelihoods.

The organization operates in more than 130 countries and continues to support initiatives related to sustainable agriculture, climate-smart farming, fisheries, forestry, rural infrastructure, nutrition, and agribusiness development.

Working at FAO gives professionals access to global networks, international collaborations, policy engagement opportunities, and hands-on development work that impacts millions of lives worldwide.

Overview of the Investment Support Officer Position

The Investment Support Officer role is based in Accra, Ghana and falls under the FAO Investment Centre Division (CFI). The position is classified at the P-3 professional level and is offered as a fixed-term appointment for one year with the possibility of extension depending on organizational needs and performance.

Position Details

  • Organization: Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO)
  • Job Title: Investment Support Officer
  • Job Number: 2601055
  • Duty Station: Accra, Ghana
  • Grade Level: P-3
  • Job Type: Professional Staff Position
  • Contract Duration: One year with possibility of extension
  • Organizational Unit: CFI

Responsibilities of the Investment Support Officer

The selected candidate will support FAO’s investment operations and agricultural development activities. The role focuses on strengthening investment planning, project implementation, technical coordination, and stakeholder engagement within agricultural and rural development sectors.

Key responsibilities may include:

  • Supporting the preparation and implementation of agricultural investment projects
  • Assisting governments and development partners in project formulation
  • Conducting policy analysis and investment assessments
  • Coordinating with international financial institutions and development agencies
  • Supporting rural development and agribusiness initiatives
  • Contributing to sustainable agricultural financing strategies
  • Monitoring project progress and preparing technical reports
  • Providing technical assistance related to food systems and investment programs

Professionals in this role are expected to work closely with multidisciplinary teams and contribute to development projects that align with FAO’s broader mission of ending hunger and promoting sustainable agriculture.

Why This Opportunity is Important

This role provides exposure to international development systems and offers practical experience in agricultural investment support at a global level. Working with FAO allows professionals to gain valuable expertise in:

  • International development cooperation
  • Agricultural economics and policy
  • Sustainable food systems
  • Rural financing and investment
  • Multilateral project management
  • UN operational systems
  • Stakeholder and donor engagement

The position also allows professionals to contribute to the achievement of the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), particularly those related to hunger eradication, poverty reduction, economic growth, and climate resilience.

Benefits of Working with FAO

FAO jobs are highly competitive because of the organization’s strong global reputation and career development opportunities. Selected candidates may benefit from:

  • International UN work experience
  • Competitive salary and benefits
  • Exposure to global agricultural and development policies
  • Networking with international experts and institutions
  • Career advancement opportunities within the UN system
  • Professional growth through international projects
  • Participation in impactful development initiatives

FAO also emphasizes workforce diversity and encourages applications from women, underrepresented nationalities, and persons with disabilities.

Who Should Apply?

This opportunity is ideal for professionals with backgrounds in:

  • Agricultural economics
  • Development finance
  • Agribusiness
  • Rural development
  • International development
  • Economics
  • Public policy
  • Investment analysis
  • Food systems
  • Project management

Applicants with previous experience in development organizations, government institutions, NGOs, international agencies, or agricultural investment projects may have a competitive advantage.

Application Process

Interested applicants must apply through the official FAO careers portal. Applications submitted outside the official system may not be considered.

Candidates are generally required to:

  1. Create an account on the FAO recruitment portal
  2. Complete their candidate profile
  3. Upload required documents
  4. Submit their application before the deadline

Applicants are advised to tailor their CV and application materials carefully to match the job description and highlight relevant international development or agricultural investment experience.

Deadline

10 June 2026.

Final Thoughts

The FAO Investment Support Officer position in Ghana represents an excellent opportunity for professionals seeking impactful international careers within the United Nations system. With growing global attention on food security, sustainable agriculture, and economic resilience, professionals in agricultural investment and rural development are increasingly in demand.

This role not only provides career growth and international exposure but also offers the chance to contribute meaningfully to global development goals and sustainable food systems transformation.

APPLY HERE

For more information on this opportunity, click here.

For more global opportunities, click here.

The post FAO Investment Support Officer Job in Ghana 2026: Fully Detailed Guide for International Applicants appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: BELIEVE in Climate Action Internships 2026: Fully Funded Youth Climate Leadership Opportunity in the UK
item.dc:creator: Akua Ntim
item.description:
Young people passionate about climate action, sustainability, leadership, and community transformation now have an exciting opportunity to become part of a groundbreaking environmental initiative in the United Kingdom. The BELIEVE in Climate Action Internship Programme 2026, led by the Faith & Belief Forum, is officially accepting applications for its first cohort of interns. This innovative […]

The post BELIEVE in Climate Action Internships 2026: Fully Funded Youth Climate Leadership Opportunity in the UK appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Young people passionate about climate action, sustainability, leadership, and community transformation now have an exciting opportunity to become part of a groundbreaking environmental initiative in the United Kingdom. The BELIEVE in Climate Action Internship Programme 2026, led by the Faith & Belief Forum, is officially accepting applications for its first cohort of interns.

This innovative climate leadership initiative is designed to empower young people from underrepresented communities to actively engage in climate action, sustainability projects, and environmental leadership at both local and national levels. Supported by significant funding from The National Lottery Community Fund, the programme aims to create a more inclusive climate movement by amplifying voices that are often underrepresented in environmental spaces.

The BELIEVE project is not just another internship programme. It is a long-term movement focused on reshaping how young people from faith communities and diverse cultural backgrounds contribute to climate solutions in the UK.

What is the BELIEVE in Climate Action Project?

BELIEVE is a multi-year climate initiative funded through The National Lottery Community Fund’s Climate Action Fund. The project received over £1.4 million in funding to support climate engagement and leadership development among young people from minority and underrepresented communities.

The initiative is spearheaded by the Faith & Belief Forum in partnership with the University of Cambridge and several climate-focused organisations and researchers. Together, they aim to create a new model for inclusive and community-driven environmental action.

The programme focuses on connecting faith, values, identity, and climate action. It recognizes that environmental sustainability is not only a scientific or political issue but also a moral and social responsibility that requires participation from all sectors of society.

According to the programme organisers, many young people from minority faiths and ethnic communities are deeply concerned about climate change but are often excluded from climate leadership opportunities. BELIEVE seeks to close this gap by creating pathways for participation, mentorship, training, and real-world action.

What Will Interns Do?

Selected interns will participate in climate-focused leadership activities and community engagement projects designed to make a measurable impact.

Interns may be involved in:

  • Supporting school-based climate action clubs
  • Participating in environmental education initiatives
  • Co-leading sustainability and climate awareness campaigns
  • Working with community organisations and policymakers
  • Engaging in interfaith dialogue around climate justice
  • Collaborating with youth leaders and environmental advocates
  • Developing leadership, communication, and advocacy skills
  • Participating in mentoring and professional development activities

The project also intends to connect interns with national and regional climate networks, allowing them to expand their professional opportunities and contribute meaningfully to environmental discussions.

Why This Internship Matters

Climate change continues to disproportionately affect vulnerable and marginalized communities worldwide. However, many environmental spaces still lack diversity and inclusive representation.

The BELIEVE Internship Programme addresses this challenge directly by ensuring that young people from different faiths, ethnic backgrounds, and communities have access to climate leadership opportunities.

The programme places strong emphasis on:

  • Inclusion and representation
  • Youth empowerment
  • Community-led environmental action
  • Interfaith collaboration
  • Sustainability education
  • Leadership development
  • Social impact

This approach allows interns to contribute to climate solutions while also strengthening relationships across diverse communities.

Benefits of the BELIEVE Internship Programme

Participants selected for the internship programme can gain several valuable benefits, including:

1. Leadership Development

Interns will build practical leadership and communication skills through hands-on environmental projects and mentorship.

2. Climate Action Experience

Participants will gain direct exposure to climate campaigns, sustainability initiatives, and environmental advocacy work.

3. Networking Opportunities

Interns will connect with researchers, policymakers, educators, environmental organisations, and fellow youth leaders across the UK.

4. Professional Mentorship

The programme includes training and mentoring opportunities to help young people grow personally and professionally.

5. Community Impact

Interns will actively contribute to meaningful climate projects within schools and local communities.

6. Exposure to National Climate Conversations

Participants may engage with broader climate policy discussions and collaborative environmental initiatives.

Areas of Focus

The first phase of the programme will begin in selected regions across England, including:

  • London
  • Northwest England
  • West Midlands
  • East of England

The project is expected to expand to additional regions in subsequent years.

Partner Organisations

The BELIEVE project involves collaboration between multiple respected institutions and organisations, including:

  • Faith & Belief Forum
  • University of Cambridge
  • Cambridge Interfaith Programme
  • London School of Economics (LSE)
  • Faith for the Climate
  • Ashden
  • Youth Environmental Service

These partnerships help provide research expertise, climate education support, mentorship, and community engagement opportunities.

Who Should Apply?

The internship is ideal for young individuals who are:

  • Passionate about climate justice and sustainability
  • Interested in leadership and advocacy
  • Motivated to create positive social impact
  • Interested in interfaith collaboration and dialogue
  • Eager to work on community-based environmental initiatives

Applicants from minority faith groups, ethnic minority backgrounds, migrant communities, and multilingual communities are strongly encouraged to apply.

Application Deadline

17 June 2026

Interested applicants are encouraged to apply early and carefully review all application requirements before submission.

APPLY HERE

For more information on this opportunity, click here.

For more global opportunities, click here.

The post BELIEVE in Climate Action Internships 2026: Fully Funded Youth Climate Leadership Opportunity in the UK appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: VentureWell Ocean Enterprise Accelerator 2026: Funding, Mentorship, and Commercialization Support for Blue Economy Innovators
item.dc:creator: Akua Ntim
item.description:
The VentureWell Ocean Enterprise Accelerator 2026 is now accepting applications for innovators and startups working to transform the future of ocean technology, marine innovation, and coastal resilience. Backed by the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA), this national accelerator program is designed to support entrepreneurs and innovators who are building groundbreaking solutions that strengthen the […]

The post VentureWell Ocean Enterprise Accelerator 2026: Funding, Mentorship, and Commercialization Support for Blue Economy Innovators appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The VentureWell Ocean Enterprise Accelerator 2026 is now accepting applications for innovators and startups working to transform the future of ocean technology, marine innovation, and coastal resilience. Backed by the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA), this national accelerator program is designed to support entrepreneurs and innovators who are building groundbreaking solutions that strengthen the blue economy in the United States.

As global attention increasingly shifts toward sustainable oceans, marine technology, climate resilience, and coastal infrastructure, the Ocean Enterprise Accelerator presents an exceptional opportunity for innovators to receive funding, expert mentorship, entrepreneurial training, and commercialization support. The accelerator aims to help early-stage ventures move from innovative concepts to scalable businesses with real-world impact.

Applications for the Fall 2026 Stage 1 cohort are currently open, and selected participants will gain access to a powerful ecosystem of investors, industry experts, academic institutions, and commercialization resources.

What Is the VentureWell Ocean Enterprise Accelerator?

The VentureWell Ocean Enterprise Accelerator is a multi-stage national accelerator program focused on supporting technologies and innovations that address critical ocean and coastal challenges. The initiative seeks to empower startups and innovators who are developing ocean-related technologies, products, and services that can contribute to economic growth, environmental sustainability, and resilient coastal communities.

The accelerator is structured to help innovators progress through different phases of venture development. Over four years, the program plans to support approximately 220 ventures through a carefully designed four-stage development pipeline.

Participants benefit from:

  • Entrepreneurial training
  • Personalized mentorship and coaching
  • Technology Development and Commercialization (TDC) funding awards
  • Access to technical experts
  • Networking opportunities
  • Business model development support
  • Market validation assistance
  • Commercialization pathways
  • Industry and academic partnerships
  • Access to prototyping and testing facilities

The program specifically focuses on strengthening the U.S. blue economy while encouraging innovation in marine and coastal sectors.

Areas of Innovation Supported

The Ocean Enterprise Accelerator welcomes innovators working across a wide range of marine and coastal technology sectors. Applicants developing technologies in the following areas are encouraged to apply:

  • Coastal management
  • Marine carbon technologies
  • Ocean data technologies
  • Water quality solutions
  • Aquaculture innovation
  • Maritime robotics
  • Shipping and ports technology
  • Ocean sensors
  • Climate resilience solutions
  • Coastal infrastructure
  • Ocean modeling technologies
  • Marine tourism innovation
  • Environmental monitoring systems
  • Autonomous underwater systems

The accelerator is particularly interested in technologies that can be commercialized and scaled for societal and economic benefit.

Understanding the Accelerator Stages

Stage 0: Introductory Innovation Program

Stage 0 serves as an optional introductory pathway for innovators who are new to commercialization and venture development. This stage consists of three half-day virtual workshops designed to expose participants to startup methodologies and commercialization strategies.

Participants in Stage 0 will:

  • Learn lean startup principles
  • Understand commercialization pathways
  • Explore market opportunities
  • Network with experts and peers
  • Discover funding opportunities
  • Receive guidance on venture development

Although Stage 0 is optional, it is highly recommended for innovators who are at the early exploration phase of their entrepreneurial journey.

Stage 1: Venture Development and Commercialization

Stage 1 is the primary accelerator stage currently open for applications. This intensive program includes seven weeks of virtual training combined with a three-day in-person workshop.

Participants in Stage 1 receive:

  • Personalized coaching
  • Business model development support
  • Market discovery and validation training
  • Technical development planning
  • Access to mentors and industry experts
  • Commercialization guidance

One of the most significant benefits of Stage 1 is eligibility for the $15,000 Technology Development and Commercialization (TDC) award. These non-dilutive awards are provided through a competitive merit review process.

The Fall 2026 in-person workshop will be hosted in New Orleans.

Stage 2 and Stage 3 Opportunities

Participants who successfully complete Stage 1 can advance into Stage 2 and Stage 3 of the accelerator.

Additional benefits include:

  • Advanced mentorship
  • Investor readiness preparation
  • Venture scaling support
  • Market entry assistance
  • Additional commercialization guidance

Funding opportunities increase significantly at higher stages:

  • Stage 2: Up to $50,000 TDC award
  • Stage 3: Up to $75,000 TDC award

Across all stages, ventures may access cumulative support of up to $140,000.

Why This Accelerator Matters

The blue economy continues to emerge as one of the fastest-growing innovation sectors globally. From climate resilience and marine conservation to ocean robotics and coastal infrastructure, there is increasing demand for scalable technologies that support sustainable ocean development.

The VentureWell Ocean Enterprise Accelerator addresses a major gap in commercialization support for ocean innovators by combining funding, mentorship, entrepreneurial training, and industry access within one structured ecosystem.

For founders and innovators working in ocean-related sectors, this accelerator can significantly improve venture readiness, market validation, and investor visibility.

Key Dates for the Fall 2026 Stage 1 Cohort

Important dates applicants should note include:

  • Information Session: May 18, 2026
  • Application Deadline: July 7, 2026
  • Virtual Training: September 23 – November 4, 2026
  • In-Person Sessions: November 9 – 11, 2026

Applicants are encouraged to prepare their applications early to maximize their chances of selection.

Who Should Apply?

This accelerator is ideal for:

  • Ocean technology startups
  • Blue economy innovators
  • Researchers commercializing marine technologies
  • Students with scalable ocean solutions
  • Entrepreneurs working in coastal resilience
  • Innovators building maritime technologies
  • Small businesses focused on marine sustainability

Applicants do not need to be fully established companies, but they should demonstrate strong innovation potential and commitment to commercialization.

Final Thoughts

The VentureWell Ocean Enterprise Accelerator 2026 represents a major opportunity for innovators building the future of ocean and coastal technologies. With substantial funding opportunities, expert mentorship, entrepreneurial training, and access to commercialization resources, the program is positioned to help transform promising ideas into scalable ventures with meaningful impact.

As ocean sustainability, climate adaptation, and blue economy innovation continue to gain importance globally, programs like the Ocean Enterprise Accelerator are becoming critical platforms for emerging founders and researchers.

Deadline
July 7, 2026.

APPLY HERE

For more information on this opportunity, click here.

For more global opportunities, click here

The post VentureWell Ocean Enterprise Accelerator 2026: Funding, Mentorship, and Commercialization Support for Blue Economy Innovators appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: IWA LeaP Leadership Programme 2026 for Young Water Professionals: Fully Funded Global Leadership Opportunity
item.dc:creator: Akua Ntim
item.description:
The International Water Association (IWA) has officially opened applications for the second cohort of its highly prestigious LeaP Leadership Programme for Young Water Professionals. This transformative global initiative is designed to empower emerging leaders in the water sector by strengthening their leadership abilities, strategic thinking, professional networks, and global impact. The IWA LeaP Leadership Programme […]

The post IWA LeaP Leadership Programme 2026 for Young Water Professionals: Fully Funded Global Leadership Opportunity appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The International Water Association (IWA) has officially opened applications for the second cohort of its highly prestigious LeaP Leadership Programme for Young Water Professionals. This transformative global initiative is designed to empower emerging leaders in the water sector by strengthening their leadership abilities, strategic thinking, professional networks, and global impact.

The IWA LeaP Leadership Programme is more than a professional development initiative. It is a life-changing leadership journey that equips talented young water professionals with the tools, confidence, and international exposure needed to tackle some of the world’s most pressing water and sanitation challenges.

Applications are currently open for the 2026 cohort, and selected participants will engage in an immersive 12-month leadership experience alongside peers from different regions and professional backgrounds.

About the IWA LeaP Leadership Programme

The International Water Association created the LeaP Leadership Programme to nurture the next generation of water leaders across the globe. The programme brings together 12 exceptional young professionals who are passionate about sustainable water management, innovation, sanitation, environmental protection, and achieving Sustainable Development Goal 6 (SDG 6).

Participants are selected from diverse countries, sectors, and cultures, creating a highly collaborative and globally representative learning environment.

According to the International Water Association, the programme is designed to help participants:

  • Develop transformational leadership skills
  • Build confidence and strategic thinking capacity
  • Strengthen global professional networks
  • Learn to navigate complex and rapidly changing environments
  • Increase their visibility and impact within the water sector
  • Become influential leaders capable of driving positive change in their communities and organizations

The programme combines experiential learning, mentorship, coaching, collaborative projects, and international networking opportunities.

What Participants Will Gain

Selected fellows will benefit from an intensive and highly rewarding leadership experience that goes beyond traditional academic or technical training.

Some of the major benefits include:

1. Leadership Development

Participants will explore their leadership identity, purpose, strengths, and vision. The programme focuses on helping young professionals become confident and impactful leaders capable of influencing policies, organizations, and communities.

2. Global Networking Opportunities

One of the strongest aspects of the programme is the opportunity to connect with young water professionals from across the world. Participants will build long-term relationships with experts, innovators, mentors, and fellow emerging leaders.

3. International Exposure

Participants will gain global exposure through collaboration with international experts and attendance at major IWA events.

4. Immersive Leadership Retreat

The programme includes a four-day immersive retreat in a remote setting where participants engage in leadership exercises, collaborative problem-solving, personal reflection, and project development.

Previous retreats have focused on resilience, sustainability, equity, and water management challenges affecting communities globally.

5. Attendance at the 2026 IWA World Water Congress & Exhibition

Selected fellows will attend the prestigious IWA World Water Congress & Exhibition in Glasgow, Scotland. This global gathering connects thousands of water professionals, researchers, policymakers, and innovators from around the world.

6. Coaching and Mentorship

Participants receive ongoing mentorship, coaching sessions, virtual learning opportunities, and collaborative project support throughout the programme duration.

Fully Funded Benefits

The IWA LeaP Leadership Programme provides substantial financial support to ensure participants can fully engage in the experience regardless of their economic background.

Covered expenses include:

  • Economy-class international flights
  • Accommodation
  • Daily subsistence allowance
  • Ground transportation
  • Travel insurance
  • Visa application fees

The programme organizers aim to remove financial barriers and ensure equitable access for talented young leaders worldwide.

Programme Structure

The programme runs over 12 months and combines both virtual and in-person learning experiences.

Activities include:

  • Four-day immersive retreat
  • Monthly virtual learning sessions
  • Coaching and mentorship meetings
  • Collaborative project work
  • Leadership exercises
  • Networking events
  • Participation in international conferences and workshops

Participants are expected to dedicate approximately 10 hours per month to programme activities.

Eligibility Requirements

To apply for the IWA LeaP Leadership Programme, candidates must meet the following requirements:

  • Be between 18 and 35 years old
  • Possess strong English communication skills
  • Hold active IWA membership for at least one year
  • Demonstrate leadership experience and potential
  • Show commitment to creating positive impact in the water sector
  • Have experience in professional, volunteer, organizational, or academic leadership

Applicants from all countries and backgrounds are encouraged to apply.

Required Application Documents

Applicants must submit the following documents in English:

  • Curriculum Vitae (maximum two pages)
  • Motivation Letter
  • Reference Letter from employer or organization

Candidates are encouraged to provide detailed and impactful information demonstrating their achievements, leadership experience, and vision for the future of water management.

Why This Programme Matters

Global water challenges continue to increase due to climate change, urbanization, pollution, and population growth. The need for innovative, ethical, and visionary leadership in the water sector has never been more urgent.

The IWA LeaP Leadership Programme is investing directly in the next generation of leaders who will shape sustainable water systems, influence policy, promote equitable access to clean water, and build resilient communities.

The programme empowers young professionals to think globally while creating practical local impact.

Application Deadline

The deadline to apply for the IWA LeaP Leadership Programme 2026 is 12 June 2026.

APPLY HERE

For more information on this opportunity, click here.

For more global opportunities, click here.

The post IWA LeaP Leadership Programme 2026 for Young Water Professionals: Fully Funded Global Leadership Opportunity appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Youth Impact Research Manager (South Africa) — Full Website Article, Social Post, Opportunity Category & Eligibility
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
Youth Impact is a globally recognized, evidence‑based international NGO dedicated to improving education and health outcomes for young people. Through rigorous research, strong government partnerships, and scalable program models, Youth Impact works across Botswana, South Africa, Namibia, India, and the Philippines, with additional partnerships worldwide. The organization is entering a new phase of rapid global […]

The post Youth Impact Research Manager (South Africa) — Full Website Article, Social Post, Opportunity Category & Eligibility appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Youth Impact is a globally recognized, evidence‑based international NGO dedicated to improving education and health outcomes for young people. Through rigorous research, strong government partnerships, and scalable program models, Youth Impact works across Botswana, South Africa, Namibia, India, and the Philippines, with additional partnerships worldwide. The organization is entering a new phase of rapid global expansion, strengthening its capacity to deliver high‑impact programs through public systems.

South Africa is one of Youth Impact’s most dynamic Innovation Hubs, and the organization is now recruiting a Research Manager to support its growing portfolio of programs, research activities, and government partnerships.

About Youth Impact’s Work in South Africa

South Africa plays a central role in Youth Impact’s Africa‑wide scale‑up strategy. The country team leads the implementation of Teaching at the Right Level (TaRL) in partnership with the National Department of Basic Education. The program currently reaches learners across Gauteng, North West, KwaZulu‑Natal, and Limpopo.

As an Innovation Hub, the South Africa team:

  • Implements multiple education programs
  • Conducts direct delivery alongside government partners
  • Generates evidence that informs global scale‑up
  • Tests and refines program models to maximize impact

This environment requires strong leadership, robust systems, and a deep understanding of evidence‑based program delivery.

About the Research Manager Role

The Research Manager will provide strategic and operational leadership at a pivotal moment in Youth Impact’s growth in South Africa. The role is designed for a professional who understands evidence, values data‑driven decision‑making, and can build a strong voice within South Africa’s education and research community.

The Research Manager will sit within the program team, ensuring that evidence is tightly connected to implementation. They will also work closely with the Research and Innovation team and report to the South Africa Country Director.

This position offers significant growth potential as the South Africa hub expands.

Primary Responsibilities

1. Team Management

  • Directly manage staff working on programs, research, and monitoring and evaluation
  • Build institutional structures and management systems as the team grows
  • Serve as a senior leadership presence for day‑to‑day decision‑making
  • Support the Country Director in broader team management as the role evolves

2. Research Oversight and Translation

  • Oversee research and M&E activities in collaboration with the Research and Innovation team
  • Build systems that support efficient data flows and program delivery
  • Quality‑check data outputs and escalate issues when necessary
  • Translate research findings into actionable insights for program teams and government partners
  • Support the design of data collection tools and M&E systems tailored to the South African context

3. Program Oversight

  • Ensure programs are implemented with fidelity to their evidence base
  • Coordinate across implementation teams to maintain quality and timelines
  • Oversee training schedules, school implementation calendars, and field logistics
  • Embed operational systems suited to a rapidly growing hub
  • Contribute to grant applications, funder reports, and program documentation

4. Stakeholder Engagement

  • Represent Youth Impact within South Africa’s research and education community
  • Engage with government partners, including national and provincial departments
  • Collaborate with research partners such as J‑PAL Africa
  • Build and maintain relationships with funders and key stakeholders
  • Support development of policy‑friendly evidence products for South African audiences

Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in education, public policy, economics, or a related field
  • Minimum 7 years of experience in program management, M&E, or related roles
  • Experience in the South African education sector is advantageous
  • Familiarity with South Africa’s research or development landscape
  • Existing networks in the research community are beneficial

Who Will Thrive in This Role

  • Strong manager: Proven ability to lead teams and develop others
  • Data‑driven: Skilled in interpreting and communicating evidence
  • Systems builder: Able to create structure in fast‑moving environments
  • Effective communicator: Clear communicator across government, research, and implementation partners
  • Positive and resilient: Adaptive, collaborative, and able to deliver results

Position Details

  • Location: South Africa (with travel to Gauteng, North West, KwaZulu‑Natal, and Limpopo)
  • Contract: 2‑year fixed term, renewable based on performance
  • Start date: As soon as possible
  • Application deadline: Rolling applications.
  • Earlybird deadline: 14 June 2026

Benefits

Youth Impact offers:

  • Generous annual leave
  • Sick leave and global health insurance provisions
  • Professional development opportunities
  • Remote work flexibility
  • Access to global networks
  • Additional stipends and organizational benefits

Apply here

For more information about this opportunity visit here

Discover more job opportunities here 

The post Youth Impact Research Manager (South Africa) — Full Website Article, Social Post, Opportunity Category & Eligibility appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Digital Democracy Centre x TrygFonden Fellowship Program 2027: A Comprehensive Guide for Applicants
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
The Digital Democracy Centre (DDC) at the University of Southern Denmark has officially opened applications for the 2027 DDC x TrygFonden Fellowship Program, a prestigious initiative designed to advance interdisciplinary research and public engagement on the impact of digital technologies and artificial intelligence on democracy, media, and society. This fellowship is one of the few […]

The post Digital Democracy Centre x TrygFonden Fellowship Program 2027: A Comprehensive Guide for Applicants appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Digital Democracy Centre (DDC) at the University of Southern Denmark has officially opened applications for the 2027 DDC x TrygFonden Fellowship Program, a prestigious initiative designed to advance interdisciplinary research and public engagement on the impact of digital technologies and artificial intelligence on democracy, media, and society. This fellowship is one of the few global programs that intentionally brings together researchers, journalists, and policymakers to co-create knowledge, strengthen democratic resilience, and shape the future of digital governance.

The program is supported by TrygFonden, one of Denmark’s major philanthropic foundations, and is anchored within DDC’s mission to explore how digitalization and AI influence political communication, public trust, media systems, and democratic processes. According to the document, the fellowship “fosters collaboration between researchers, journalists, and policymakers in exploring the impact of digital technology and AI on media, politics, and democracy” .

Program Overview

The DDC x TrygFonden Fellowship Program is designed to generate new knowledge, strengthen networks, and produce tangible outputs such as:

  • Policy briefs
  • Educational packages
  • Public talks
  • Outreach activities
  • Collaborative research
  • Practical tools for institutions, NGOs, and media organizations

Fellows work closely with a self-selected DDC mentor, who must confirm mentorship before the application is submitted. As stated in the document, “Applications without a confirmed mentor will automatically be rejected” .

The program welcomes three types of fellows:

  1. Academic fellows researching digital technologies and democracy
  2. Journalist fellows covering digital democracy topics
  3. Policy fellows contributing to regulatory debates and practical implementation

Projects with relevance to Denmark or those offering comparative insights applicable to Danish society are prioritized. In 2027, projects connected to the Hybrid Warfare Initiative—which examines societal resilience in the face of new international conflict forms—will receive additional priority.

About the Digital Democracy Centre (DDC)

Founded in 2021 at the University of Southern Denmark, the DDC is a leading interdisciplinary hub dedicated to understanding how digital technologies and AI shape democratic life. The document notes that digitalization “affects the very core of democracy” and raises questions about microtargeting, fake news, algorithmic decision-making, and trust in information .

DDC’s research spans:

  • Digital Democratic Integrity
  • Digital Democracy & Societal Transition
  • AI, Media & Journalism
  • Citizens, Organizations & New Technologies

The Centre includes approximately 50 researchers across fields such as:

  • Computer science
  • Journalism
  • Political science
  • Media studies
  • Law
  • Economics
  • Communication

DDC leadership includes prominent scholars such as Professor Claes de Vreese, Associate Professor Lena Frischlich, Associate Professor Curd Knüpfer, and Professor Josphine Lukito.

Purpose of the Fellowship Program

The fellowship aims to:

  • Expand DDC’s knowledge base
  • Strengthen collaboration between DDC, TrygFonden, and external partners
  • Produce new interdisciplinary insights
  • Support public debate in Denmark and beyond
  • Create practical outputs such as policy briefs, educational tools, and public talks

Fellows gain access to:

  • A dedicated DDC mentor
  • Research resources
  • Flexible workspaces
  • Speaker series and networks
  • Financial support (salary subsidy or stipend)
  • The DDC alumni network

The document states that benefits “may include financial support, access to DDC’s research resources, flexible working spaces, speaker series, and networks” .

Expectations for Fellows

Fellows are expected to:

  • Engage actively in DDC activities
  • Collaborate with other fellows and researchers
  • Participate in seminars, workshops, and public outreach
  • Coordinate stays in Odense, Denmark, aligned with the Danish working calendar
  • Submit a short report within two months after completing the fellowship

Fellows must also secure support from their home institution or employer before applying.

Practical Structure of the Fellowship

  • Duration: Typically 2–6 months (longer possible in exceptional cases)
  • Start Date: Any time in 2027
  • Work Format: Combination of remote work and in-person stays in Odense
  • Financial Support: Salary subsidy or stipend (applicants must specify needs)
  • Collaboration: Fellows work with a mentor and external partners such as NGOs, media organizations, ministries, schools, or consumer organizations

Application Requirements

Applicants must submit:

  1. Preliminary project title
  2. Fellowship type (academic, journalist, or policy)
  3. Brief project description (max 1000 characters)
  4. Detailed description & timeline (max 2400 characters), including:
    • Fellowship duration (minimum two months)
    • Start date within Danish working times
    • Confirmed mentor
    • Work plan (remote vs. in-office)
  5. Description of collaboration with external partners (max 1000 characters)
  6. Expected outputs (max 500 characters)
  7. CV (max 5 pages)
  8. Budget overview (flights, housing, research costs, salary compensation, etc.)

Applications must be written in English.

Deadline

Application Deadline: September 1, 2026 Decisions will be communicated by October 1, 2026.

For more information about this opportunity ad to apply visit here

Discover more fellowship  opportunities here 

The post Digital Democracy Centre x TrygFonden Fellowship Program 2027: A Comprehensive Guide for Applicants appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Pulitzer Center Announces Applications Open for the 2026–2027 AI Accountability Fellowships
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
The Pulitzer Center has officially opened applications for its fifth cohort of the AI Accountability Fellowships, a prestigious global program supporting journalists investigating the real‑world impact of artificial intelligence. As AI systems continue to shape governments, corporations, workplaces, and everyday life, the need for rigorous, human‑centered reporting has never been more urgent. This Fellowship empowers […]

The post Pulitzer Center Announces Applications Open for the 2026–2027 AI Accountability Fellowships appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Pulitzer Center has officially opened applications for its fifth cohort of the AI Accountability Fellowships, a prestigious global program supporting journalists investigating the real‑world impact of artificial intelligence. As AI systems continue to shape governments, corporations, workplaces, and everyday life, the need for rigorous, human‑centered reporting has never been more urgent.

This Fellowship empowers journalists to pursue ambitious, high‑impact investigations into how AI systems are designed, deployed, and governed—especially in communities where oversight is weak and harms are often overlooked.

Deadline: July 12, 2026

About the AI Accountability Fellowships

Launched in 2022, the AI Accountability Fellowships have become a cornerstone of global AI journalism. Over four years, the program has supported 35 journalists across five continents, enabling groundbreaking investigations that have influenced legislation, informed lawsuits, and sparked civic engagement.

The 2026–2027 cohort will continue this mission by supporting 8–10 journalists worldwide who are committed to uncovering the human, social, political, and economic implications of AI systems.

Why This Fellowship Matters

AI is no longer a niche topic. It is embedded in:

  • Government decision‑making
  • Corporate operations
  • Labor systems
  • Social welfare programs
  • Surveillance and security
  • Online platforms and digital ecosystems

As these technologies expand, so do the risks—bias, exclusion, exploitation, opacity, and unchecked power. The Fellowship equips journalists to investigate these issues with depth, nuance, and global perspective.

What the Fellowship Offers

Selected Fellows receive up to $25,000 in total support:

  1. Up to $20,000 for reporting
    • Travel
    • Data analysis
    • Records requests
    • Research support
    • Stipends
  2. Up to $5,000 for engagement activities
    • Community outreach
    • Public events
    • Partnerships with creators
    • Zines, workshops, or local dissemination
    • Multi‑stakeholder dialogues
  3. Mentorship and Training
    • Access to experts across AI, policy, data, and investigative journalism
    • Monthly virtual training sessions
    • Peer learning with a global cohort
  4. A Collaborative Global Community
    • Monthly 1.5–2 hour mandatory meetings
    • Participation in community calls
    • Engagement on the Fellowship’s online platform
  5. Impact‑Driven Storytelling Support For the first time, Fellows will receive structured guidance to design and execute an impact plan—ensuring their reporting reaches the audiences who can act on it.

Examples of Past Investigations

Previous Fellows have produced influential reporting on:

  • Data labelers unknowingly working for military projects
  • Big Tech’s strategies for global data center expansion
  • Algorithmic flaws in Peru’s poverty‑targeting system
  • Export of facial recognition tools to countries with weak regulations
  • AI‑driven welfare fraud detection in Europe

These stories have led to:

  • New legislation in Brazil
  • Evidence used in legal cases
  • Student‑led investigations in Indonesia
  • Creative civic engagement, including poetry and community storytelling

Fellowship Requirements

To maintain a strong, collaborative learning environment, Fellows must:

  • Attend monthly mandatory meetings (1.5–2 hours)
  • Join at least one community call
  • Participate actively in virtual discussions
  • Share methodologies and lessons learned
  • Engage in peer review and collaborative learning

If a journalist has a strong story but cannot commit to these requirements, the Pulitzer Center recommends applying for an AI Reporting Grant instead.

Program Structure and Timeline

  • Fellowship Duration: 10 months
  • Start Date: September 2026
  • Deadline to Apply: July 12, 2026

Additional Programs Under the AI Accountability Network

The Fellowship is part of the Pulitzer Center’s broader AI Accountability Network, launched in 2022 to diversify and strengthen global AI reporting.

Other programs include:

  • AI Reporting Grants – Short‑term support for in‑depth AI stories
  • AI Spotlight Series – Free online training in English, Spanish, and Portuguese
  • South to South AI Accountability CoLab – Strengthening civil society and journalism collaboration in Africa, Latin America, and South/Southeast Asia

The Network is supported by major philanthropic organizations including the MacArthur Foundation, Open Society Foundations, Ford Foundation, Luminate, Omidyar Network, and others.

How to Apply

Applicants are encouraged to:

  • Review the Fellowship details
  • Explore past reporting
  • Attend an AI Spotlight Series training session
  • Submit a complete application before the deadline

Deadline: July 12, 2026

Apply here

For more information about this opportunity visit here

Discover more fellowship opportunities here 

The post Pulitzer Center Announces Applications Open for the 2026–2027 AI Accountability Fellowships appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Emerging Leaders in AI: Graduate Prep Program 2026–2027 — Comprehensive Guide for Applicants
item.dc:creator: Sarah Kayiwa
item.description:
The Emerging Leaders in AI: Graduate Prep Program (2026–2027) is a highly supportive academic preparation initiative designed for aspiring graduate students who plan to apply to AI‑related Master’s and Ph.D. programs during the 2026–2027 admissions cycle. This program is hosted by Black in AI and aims to equip applicants with the knowledge, structure, mentorship, and […]

The post Emerging Leaders in AI: Graduate Prep Program 2026–2027 — Comprehensive Guide for Applicants appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Emerging Leaders in AI: Graduate Prep Program (2026–2027) is a highly supportive academic preparation initiative designed for aspiring graduate students who plan to apply to AI‑related Master’s and Ph.D. programs during the 2026–2027 admissions cycle. This program is hosted by Black in AI and aims to equip applicants with the knowledge, structure, mentorship, and community needed to submit strong and competitive graduate school applications.

The application deadline for the program is June 21, 2026 (Anywhere on Earth — AoE).

Program Overview

The Emerging Leaders in AI Graduate Prep Program is built for individuals who intend to apply to graduate programs in fields connected to artificial intelligence. These may include computer science, machine learning, data science, robotics, computational neuroscience, and other AI‑adjacent disciplines.

Participants must be planning to submit their graduate school applications between October 2026 and March 2027. The program does not fund application‑related expenses such as GRE fees, TOEFL fees, or university application fees, so applicants must have a plan to cover these costs independently.

What the Program Offers

The program provides a wide range of structured support resources to help applicants prepare competitive graduate school applications. These include:

1. Graduate Application Support Materials

Participants receive access to curated documents, guides, and resources that explain:

  • How to structure a strong Statement of Purpose
  • How to request impactful recommendation letters
  • How to build a compelling CV for graduate school
  • How to identify suitable programs and advisors

2. Community‑Based Peer Engagement

The program fosters a collaborative environment where participants can:

  • Connect with peers applying to similar programs
  • Share insights, challenges, and strategies
  • Build long‑term academic and professional networks

3. Facilitated Large‑Group Sessions

These sessions are designed to help applicants understand:

  • The graduate admissions process
  • How to prepare competitive applications
  • How to manage timelines and deadlines
  • How to navigate program selection

4. One‑to‑One Mentorship Opportunities

Participants may receive personalized mentorship from experienced scholars and professionals in AI. Mentorship may include:

  • Reviewing application materials
  • Providing feedback on research interests
  • Offering guidance on program selection
  • Preparing for interviews

5. Application Fee Waiver Verification

The program may assist with:

  • Membership verification for institutions that offer fee waivers
  • Guidance on how to request fee waivers from universities

6. Mock Interview Preparation

Participants may receive:

  • Practice interviews
  • Feedback on communication and research articulation
  • Guidance on how to present academic and professional experiences

7. Workshop Speaker Series

The program hosts expert speakers who cover topics such as:

  • Research in AI
  • Graduate school expectations
  • Academic career pathways
  • Industry and research lab opportunities

8. Online Graduate Application Information Session

This session provides:

  • A step‑by‑step overview of the application process
  • Insights into what admissions committees look for
  • Tips for strengthening each component of the application

Important Notes for Applicants

  • The program does not cover GRE, TOEFL, or graduate application fees.
  • Applicants must have a plan to cover all personal application‑related expenses.
  • All required materials must be submitted by June 21, 2026 (AoE).
  • Late submissions will not be accepted, and no extensions will be granted.
  • Questions may be directed to academic@blackinai.org.

How to Apply

Applicants must complete the official application form and upload all required documents through the designated submission portal. The form will automatically record the applicant’s name, email, and photo associated with their Google account when files are uploaded.

For more information about this opportunity ad to apply visit here

Discover more  fellowship opportunities here

 

The post Emerging Leaders in AI: Graduate Prep Program 2026–2027 — Comprehensive Guide for Applicants appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
